title: Berlin Occupation Zones and Refugee Crisis
public_claim: Berlin Occupation Zones and Refugee Crisis is a partition-and-displacement case board for studying jurisdiction, refugee pressure, intelligence risk, and early Cold War governance.
distinctive_mechanism: It routes the Berlin case through occupation zones, jurisdictional fragmentation, refugee movement, institutional stress, intelligence exposure, and analytic lessons for divided-city governance.
use_value: It turns a historical episode into an applied case for border governance, population movement, allied administration, and security risk.
